qa_pairs.question: How can I view a list of all available images within my OpenStack project?
qa_pairs.answer: To view a list of all available images in your OpenStack project, you can use the OpenStack client command `openstack image list`. This command will display details such as the image ID, name, disk format, container format, size, and status.

qa_pairs.question: What is the command to download a specific image from OpenStack to my local machine?
qa_pairs.answer: You can download a particular image from OpenStack using the command `openstack image save --file ./<file-name-for-image>.<format> <ID>`. When using this, ensure that `<file-name-for-image>` is the desired name for your local file, `<format>` matches the disk format shown in the `openstack image list` output (e.g., qcow2), and `<ID>` is the image's unique identifier from the list.

qa_pairs.question: How do I upload a local image file to my OpenStack project?
qa_pairs.answer: To upload a local image file to your OpenStack project, use the command `openstack image create --file <path-to-local-file-image> --disk-format <format> <new-image-name>`. You'll need to specify the path to your local image file, its disk format (such as qcow2 or raw), and the new name you wish the image to have on the OpenStack dashboard. If the disk format is not specified, the `raw` format is assumed, which may cause issues if your image is in a different format.
